definition:
1. a rush , Lat. juncus, scirpus, (Herdotus Historicus), etc. 
2. a reed , used as an arrow  or javelin , (Batrachomyomachia) 
3. a place where rushes grow, a rushbed , (Odyssey by Homer) 
4. a rush-rope , and generally, a rope, cord , (Oracle quoted in Herdotus Historicus) 
5. a fence  round a garden, [variant datesAnthology Palantina 
6. a land-measure , = 2 Persian parasangs, = 60 stades, (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
